title: NOC 31300 – Nursing Coordinators and Supervisors | GTR Immigration
description: Nursing Coordinators and Supervisors under NOC 31300 are in strong demand across the healthcare system in Canada. With healthcare expansion, population aging, and increasing complexity of patient care, leadership roles within nursing are becoming essential. This occupation offers excellent salary prospects, structured regulatory pathways, and strong eligibility under Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs. For internationally experienced nursing professionals, NOC 31300 represents a stable and strategic pathway toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group31 – Professional occupations in healthSub-Major Group313 – Nursing and allied health professionalsMinor Group3130 – Nursing and allied health professionalsNOC Code31300

duties: Nursing Coordinators and Supervisors perform some or all of the following duties:
Supervise registered nurses, licensed practical nurses, and nursing personnel
Evaluate patient needs and ensure appropriate nursing care delivery
Coordinate nursing services with other healthcare departments
Ensure quality standards and compliance with administrative procedures
Develop and implement unit policies and procedures
Manage nursing unit budgets and ensure adequate supplies and equipment
Participate in recruitment, evaluation, and professional development of staff
Collaborate on research and multidisciplinary healthcare initiatives
May provide direct patient care when required
employment_requirements: Completion of an approved registered nursing or registered psychiatric nursing program
Management studies or formal education in healthcare administration may be required
Registration with a provincial regulatory body is mandatory in all provinces and territories
Registered psychiatric nurse licensing required in Manitoba, Saskatchewan, Alberta, British Columbia, and Yukon
Clinical experience as a registered nurse or psychiatric nurse is required
British Columbia College of Nurses and Midwives
College and Association of Registered Nurses of Alberta
Ordre des infirmières et infirmiers du Québec
Credential assessment and provincial registration are mandatory prior to employment.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range between $48,100 and $103,900, depending on province, experience, and facility type.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 31300 remains strong nationwide. Retirement-driven replacement demand and increasing healthcare service needs will continue to create leadership vacancies.
Internationally trained nurses with supervisory experience and Canadian registration have a competitive advantage, particularly in urban hospital systems and rural health authorities.
labour_market_demand: Nursing leadership roles are critical across:
This positive employment gap highlights strong replacement demand due to retirements and sector growth. High-demand provinces include Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Saskatchewan, and Quebec.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 31300?
Nursing Coordinators and Supervisors benefit from:
High compensation compared to many other healthcare roles
This role offers both professional recognition and long-term settlement security in Canada.
pathways: If you are an experienced nursing leader, NOC 31300 provides a direct and stable immigration pathway to Canada.
